title: Side Base Cabinets
summary: A pair of matching plywood base cabinets with mitered 1x2 door frames and applied base moulding — built as flanking pieces for a media console, but a complete standalone cabinet pair on their own.
description:
Each cabinet is a plain plywood box: two sides, a bottom, and a nailer strip, closed in with a plywood back. Build both cabinets identically side by side rather than one at a time — it's much easier to keep two carcasses square and matching than to build one, set it aside, and try to reproduce it exactly later.

A 1x2 face-frame stile runs up each front edge, with a matching 1x2 top rail, giving the door something to hang from and hiding the plywood edge. **The door frames are mitered to 45° here** — if you'd rather build simple butt-jointed frames instead, your frame measurements will differ from the cut list, since a mitered frame and a butt-jointed frame consume their stock differently for the same finished opening.

Each door is a thin plywood panel set inside its mitered 1x2 frame. Finish the pair with a strip of 2"-tall base moulding across the bottom front of both cabinets, which is what visually ties them together as a matching set.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Measure your own assembled door openings — dimensions may vary.
Mitered at 45°; measurements differ if using a butt joint instead.
Mitered at 45°; measurements differ if using a butt joint instead.

steps.title: Build both carcasses
steps.body: Build the two plywood boxes side by side from the sides, bottoms, and nailer strips, keeping them identical.

steps.title: Add bottom supports and backs
steps.body: Confirm the nailer strips are level, then nail the plywood back panel to each carcass.

steps.title: Attach the face frame
steps.body: Attach the face-frame stiles to the front edge of each cabinet with pocket holes and glue, then add finish nails.

steps.title: Add the top header
steps.body: Attach a top rail across the front of each cabinet above the face-frame stiles.

steps.title: Add the base moulding
steps.body: Miter and attach the base moulding across the bottom front of both cabinets.

steps.title: Build and hang the doors
steps.body: Miter each 1x2 door frame around its plywood panel, then hang on the cabinet.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Pair of matching side base cabinets with mitered door frames
